pattern: CALCULATE with FILTER(ALL(DimTable), condition) instead of direct column filter

conditions: FILTER wraps ALL of a dimension table
inner condition is equality or range on a single column
a direct column filter would produce same result

fixed_dax: Sales YTD = CALCULATE([Total Sales], 'Date'[Year] = 2024)

pattern: LOOKUPVALUE used inside a measure when an active relationship already exists

conditions: lookup key column matches an active relationship key pair
target table is directly related to the table being iterated

fixed_dax: Category = RELATED(Product[Category])

pattern: SELECTEDVALUE used without ISINSCOPE guard — returns blank when multiple values selected

conditions: no ISINSCOPE or HASONEVALUE guard
used in a card or label visual where blank is misleading
column is not a slicer with single-select enforced

fixed_dax: Selected Region = IF(HASONEVALUE(Geography[Region]), SELECTEDVALUE(Geography[Region]), "Multiple")

pattern: ALLSELECTED used outside a visual query context — returns ALL behavior in DAX Studio or API

conditions: measure intended for use in visual but also called from DAX Studio or REST API
no ISINSCOPE guard around ALLSELECTED branch

fixed_dax: Market Share = DIVIDE([Sales], CALCULATE([Sales], ALLSELECTED(Product[Category]))) -- add ISINSCOPE guard if called outside visuals

pattern: SWITCH(TRUE()) with overlapping conditions — first match wins, later conditions silently ignored

conditions: first argument is TRUE()
two or more conditions have overlapping ranges where broader condition appears first
narrower condition can never be reached

fixed_dax: Band = SWITCH(TRUE(), [Revenue] > 1000, "High", [Revenue] > 0, "Positive", "Zero")

pattern: EARLIER() used inside CALCULATE — EARLIER breaks when CALCULATE resets row context

conditions: EARLIER appears inside CALCULATE filter argument
not inside a nested SUMX/FILTER that re-establishes row context

fixed_dax: Running Total = VAR CurrentDate = Sales[Date] RETURN CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Date] <= CurrentDate)

pattern: TOPN returning non-deterministic rows when tied values exist and no tiebreaker column is provided

conditions: only one order-by column/measure
no secondary tiebreaker column provided
measure values can repeat across rows

fixed_dax: Top 5 Customers = TOPN(5, VALUES(Customer[CustomerName]), [Revenue], DESC, Customer[CustomerName], ASC)

pattern: CONCATENATEX with no ORDERBY clause — output string order is non-deterministic

conditions: no order-by expression provided as 4th argument
result is displayed in a card or label where order matters

fixed_dax: Product List = CONCATENATEX(VALUES(Product[Name]), Product[Name], ", ", Product[Name], ASC)

pattern: RANKX using ALL(Table) — ranks all rows globally, ignores visual filter context

conditions: ALL wraps a fact table not a dimension
rank is expected to respond to slicer or page filter context

fixed_dax: Filtered Rank = RANKX(ALLSELECTED(Customer[CustomerName]), [Revenue], , DESC, Dense)

pattern: FORMAT() used in a measure for display — causes implicit text conversion breaking aggregation

conditions: FORMAT wraps a numeric measure
result used in a visual that aggregates or sorts numerically
not used exclusively in a text card

fixed_dax: Use Power BI format pane to set number format instead of FORMAT() in DAX

pattern: YEAR(TODAY()) hardcoded in measure — breaks on calendar year change without dataset refresh
bad_dax: Current Year Sales = CALCULATE([Total Sales], 'Date'[Year] = YEAR(TODAY()))

conditions: TODAY() used inside a measure to derive year
compared to a Date table year column
no parameterization via What-If or slicer

fixed_dax: Current Year Sales = CALCULATE([Total Sales], DATESYTD('Date'[Date]))

pattern: TREATAS with mismatched column count between value table and target column list
bad_dax: Filtered Sales = CALCULATE([Sales], TREATAS(VALUES(Budget[RegionID], Budget[Year]), Geography[RegionID]))

conditions: column list in VALUES or table expression has different cardinality than target columns in TREATAS
mismatched count causes runtime error or unexpected results

fixed_dax: Filtered Sales = CALCULATE([Sales], TREATAS(VALUES(Budget[RegionID]), Geography[RegionID]))

pattern: USERPRINCIPALNAME() compared to mixed-case string — RLS silently excludes users

conditions: compared to a column without LOWER() normalization on both sides
Employee[Email] column has mixed case values in source

fixed_dax: [Security Filter] = LOWER(USERPRINCIPALNAME()) = LOWER(Employee[Email])

pattern: HASONEFILTER vs HASONEVALUE — HASONEFILTER returns TRUE for cross-filter from another visual

conditions: used where HASONEVALUE is intended
table has bidirectional or cross-filter relationships that may add implicit filters

fixed_dax: Label = IF(HASONEVALUE(Product[Category]), SELECTEDVALUE(Product[Category]), "All")

pattern: CALENDAR() start date after end date — produces empty table, breaks all time intelligence

conditions: first argument evaluates to a date later than second argument
table produces zero rows

fixed_dax: DateTable = CALENDAR(DATE(2020,1,1), DATE(2024,12,31))

pattern: DATEDIFF using fact table date columns instead of Date table — skips non-business dates inconsistently

conditions: both arguments are columns from a fact table
a marked Date table exists in the model
result is used in a measure rather than calculated column

fixed_dax: Days Open = AVERAGEX(Orders, DATEDIFF(Orders[OrderDate], Orders[CloseDate], DAY))

pattern: INT() on negative numbers truncates toward zero — differs from FLOOR() and TRUNC()

conditions: applied to a column or expression that can return negative values
business intent is floor-toward-negative-infinity not truncate-toward-zero

fixed_dax: Rounded Down = FLOOR(Sales[Margin], 1)

pattern: CONTAINSROW vs CONTAINS — argument order is different; swapping causes silent wrong results

conditions: CONTAINS used for a membership test
table and column arguments could be expressed as CONTAINSROW for clarity
argument order risk when multiple columns are involved

fixed_dax: Is VIP = CONTAINSROW(VALUES(VIPList[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID])

pattern: BLANK() used in IF as explicit false branch — differs from omitting it when downstream aggregations treat blank vs zero differently

conditions: IF explicitly returns BLANK()
measure is used in SUM or AVERAGE aggregation
AVERAGEX would skip blank rows, SUM treats blank as 0 — intent unclear

fixed_dax: Safe Margin = IF([Revenue] > 0, [Margin])

pattern: MIN/MAX on a text column — returns alphabetical min/max, not numeric, misleading in financial context

conditions: applied to a text or string column
column contains numeric-looking values stored as text
result is used as a date or numeric threshold

fixed_dax: Latest Invoice = MAX(Finance[InvoiceDate]) -- convert InvoiceNumber to integer for numeric max

pattern: RELATED() called in a measure context — RELATED only works in row context (calculated columns or iterators)

conditions: RELATED used directly in a measure body
not wrapped inside SUMX, AVERAGEX, or other row-context iterator

fixed_dax: Category Revenue = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Amount] * RELATED(Product[Margin]))

pattern: ISINSCOPE called on a column not present in any hierarchy or visual — always returns FALSE

conditions: column argument is not part of a matrix row/column hierarchy
visual does not drill down to that column level

fixed_dax: Subtotal Label = IF(ISINSCOPE(Customer[City]), "Detail", "Total") -- use the column actually in the hierarchy

pattern: CALCULATE nesting with redundant inner CALCULATE — inner CALCULATE adds no additional filter
bad_dax: Scoped Sales = CALCULATE(CALCULATE([Total Sales], Product[Color] = "Red"), Product[Size] = "Large")

conditions: CALCULATE directly wraps another CALCULATE
filters in inner and outer CALCULATE operate on different columns
can be flattened into a single CALCULATE with multiple filter arguments

fixed_dax: Scoped Sales = CALCULATE([Total Sales], Product[Color] = "Red", Product[Size] = "Large")

pattern: DATESYTD with incorrect year-end argument — defaults to Dec 31, silent error for non-calendar fiscal year

conditions: no year-end date argument provided
model documentation or Date table indicates non-December fiscal year end
fiscal year context established elsewhere in the report

fixed_dax: Fiscal YTD = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESYTD('Date'[Date], "6/30"))

pattern: USERNAME() used in RLS — deprecated in Power BI Service, returns UPN format inconsistently vs USERPRINCIPALNAME()

conditions: USERNAME() used in RLS row filter
Power BI Service deployment target

fixed_dax: [RLS Filter] = USERPRINCIPALNAME() = Employee[Email]

pattern: FORMAT() inside SUMX iterator — string conversion runs on every row of the fact table

conditions: FORMAT appears inside a SUMX row-level expression
result is immediately converted back to numeric with VALUE()

fixed_dax: Formatted Total = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: SEARCH() used instead of FIND() for case-sensitive matching — SEARCH is always case-insensitive and slower

conditions: search string is all uppercase or case-specific
business intent is case-sensitive match
FIND would produce same result faster

fixed_dax: Has Error = IF(ISNUMBER(FIND("ERROR", Log[Message], 1)), 1, 0)

pattern: SUBSTITUTE() inside SUMX over a large fact table — string manipulation on every row

conditions: SUBSTITUTE appears inside a SUMX row expression
applied to a fact table with > 100k rows
result is converted to numeric

fixed_dax: Fix in Power Query: replace RevenueText with a proper numeric column. Then: Clean Revenue = SUM(Sales[Revenue])

pattern: COUNTROWS(ADDCOLUMNS(Table, ...)) — ADDCOLUMNS materializes extra columns just to count rows

conditions: COUNTROWS wraps ADDCOLUMNS
added columns are not used as filter criteria
COUNTROWS(table) would return same result

fixed_dax: Count = COUNTROWS(Sales)

pattern: SELECTCOLUMNS inside SUMX materializes unnecessary columns into the iterator scope

conditions: SELECTCOLUMNS produces more columns than the SUMX expression uses
only one of the projected columns is referenced in the SUMX body

fixed_dax: Revenue = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: GENERATE() used inside a measure — scans full table, not optimized by VertiPaq

conditions: GENERATE used inside a measure not a calculated table
produces a cross-join materialized at query time

fixed_dax: Expanded = SUMX(Orders, Orders[Amount]) -- use direct relationship traversal instead of GENERATE

pattern: CONTAINS inside FILTER over a large fact table — O(n*m) scan for every row
bad_dax: Matched = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Sales, CONTAINS(TargetList, TargetList[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])))

conditions: CONTAINS inside FILTER over a fact table
alternative relationship or TREATAS could replace membership test
TargetList has fewer rows than fact table

fixed_dax: Matched = CALCULATE([Revenue], TREATAS(VALUES(TargetList[CustomerID]), Sales[CustomerID]))

pattern: LOOKUPVALUE inside SUMX when a physical relationship exists — RELATED is 10-100x faster
bad_dax: Margin Total = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Qty] * LOOKUPVALUE(Product[Margin], Product[ProductID], Sales[ProductID]))

conditions: LOOKUPVALUE lookup keys match an active relationship
RELATED would return the same value with less overhead

fixed_dax: Margin Total = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Qty] * RELATED(Product[Margin]))

pattern: UNION of two large fact tables inside a measure — materializes both tables in memory at query time

conditions: UNION combines two fact-size tables inside a measure
result is aggregated immediately with SUMX or COUNTROWS
alternative: separate measures summed

fixed_dax: All Transactions = [Total Sales] + [Total Returns]

pattern: EXCEPT inside measure for set-difference filter — prefer FILTER with NOT IN pattern

conditions: EXCEPT inside CALCULATE filter argument
produces a virtual table that must be materialized
FILTER approach or NOT CONTAINSROW avoids full materialization

fixed_dax: Non-VIP Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), NOT CONTAINSROW(VALUES(VIPList[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID])))

pattern: DISTINCTCOUNT on concatenated text surrogate key instead of integer ID column

conditions: argument column is a text type composite key
an integer surrogate OrderID column exists in the same table
DISTINCTCOUNT on text is slower than on integer

fixed_dax: Unique Orders = DISTINCTCOUNT(Sales[OrderID])

pattern: SUMMARIZE with explicit measure columns instead of SUMMARIZECOLUMNS — SUMMARIZE is deprecated for this use

conditions: SUMMARIZE has explicit measure expressions as additional arguments beyond groupBy columns
Microsoft documentation recommends SUMMARIZECOLUMNS instead

fixed_dax: Summary = SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(Sales[Region], "Revenue", SUM(Sales[Amount]))

pattern: ALL(FactTable) used in a security context — removes RLS filters from the fact table

conditions: ALL wraps a fact table that has RLS roles defined
measure intended for market share but will show unfiltered totals to all users

fixed_dax: Total Market = CALCULATE([Revenue], REMOVEFILTERS(Sales[Region])) -- remove only the dimension filter, not RLS filter

pattern: IF inside SUMX where CALCULATE with filter argument is faster

conditions: IF inside SUMX tests a condition on a row-level column
the condition is a simple column comparison that can become a CALCULATE filter

fixed_dax: Positive Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Amount] > 0)

pattern: ADDCOLUMNS+RANKX inside CALCULATETABLE — O(n²) computation: RANKX re-evaluates for each row

conditions: RANKX inside ADDCOLUMNS inside CALCULATETABLE
RANKX re-evaluates the full domain for each row of ADDCOLUMNS

fixed_dax: Compute rank in a separate measure and reference it, or use a calculated column for static ranking.

pattern: CROSSFILTER() inside a SUMX iterator — applies relationship override on every iterator row
bad_dax: Bi-Revenue = SUMX(Customer, CALCULATE([Revenue], CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], BOTH)))

conditions: CROSSFILTER inside SUMX iterator body
CROSSFILTER could be applied at a higher scope outside the iterator

fixed_dax: Bi-Revenue = CALCULATE(SUMX(Customer, [Revenue]), CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], BOTH))

pattern: TOPN with very large N (>1000) on a fact table — materializes a large in-memory sort
bad_dax: Top Transactions = SUMX(TOPN(5000, Sales, Sales[Amount], DESC), Sales[Amount])

conditions: N argument > 1000
applied to a fact table not a dimension
result immediately aggregated — TOPN provides no benefit

fixed_dax: Top Transactions = SUM(Sales[Amount]) -- TOPN with large N on aggregated fact is equivalent to no filter

pattern: ISINSCOPE checked in a measure where the column is never part of a visual hierarchy

conditions: column argument is a surrogate key never placed in a visual row/column group
ISINSCOPE will always return FALSE — dead branch

fixed_dax: Row Type = IF(ISINSCOPE(Customer[CustomerName]), "Detail", "Total") -- use column that IS in the matrix hierarchy

pattern: DATEADD with DAY granularity over a multi-year range — evaluates many rows vs SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR

conditions: granularity is DAY
offset is -365 or +365
SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR would handle leap years correctly and is faster

fixed_dax: Prior Year = CALCULATE([Revenue], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]))

pattern: HASONEVALUE guard where SELECTEDVALUE default argument already handles the multi-value case

conditions: IF(HASONEVALUE(col), SELECTEDVALUE(col), default) pattern
SELECTEDVALUE(col, default) already returns default when multiple values selected
redundant HASONEVALUE check adds no value

fixed_dax: Selected Product = SELECTEDVALUE(Product[Name], "All Products")

pattern: PARALLELPERIOD used where DATEADD is more precise for non-month/quarter/year offsets

conditions: intent is a rolling window not a parallel calendar period
PARALLELPERIOD shifts the full current period, not a rolling window of the same length

fixed_dax: Prior 30 Days = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], MAX('Date'[Date]) - 30, -30, DAY))

pattern: SWITCH evaluating all branches when only first match matters — all SWITCH branches are evaluated in DAX
bad_dax: Tier = SWITCH(TRUE(), [Revenue] > 1000000, CALCULATE([Revenue], ALL(Sales)), [Revenue] > 100000, CALCULATE([Revenue], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date])), 0)

conditions: SWITCH branches contain expensive CALCULATE expressions
multiple branches with heavy computations even though only one will be returned
VAR caching of sub-expressions would reduce repeated evaluation

fixed_dax: VAR AllSales = CALCULATE([Revenue], ALL(Sales)) VAR PY = CALCULATE([Revenue], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date])) RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(), [Revenue] > 1000000, AllSales, [Revenue] > 100000, PY, 0)

pattern: GENERATE + ROW() pattern creating virtual single-row tables inside a measure

conditions: GENERATE and ROW combined inside a measure
creates virtual single-row table per outer row
ADDCOLUMNS achieves the same result with less overhead

fixed_dax: Debug = SUMX(ADDCOLUMNS(Sales, "Check", Sales[Amount] * 1.1), [Check])

pattern: CALCULATE with many explicit IN filters where a single table filter would be faster

conditions: three or more OR conditions on the same column
IN operator or table filter is equivalent and faster

fixed_dax: Multi-Region = CALCULATE([Revenue], Geography[Region] IN {"North", "South", "East"})

pattern: DATESYTD used without marking Date table — DAX uses auto date/time hierarchy with unpredictable results

conditions: date argument is a column from a fact table
no marked Date table in the model
auto date/time is enabled in Power BI Desktop

fixed_dax: YTD = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESYTD('Date'[Date])) -- create and mark a proper Date table first

pattern: Fact table used as lookup dimension — RELATED from one fact to another fact without bridge

conditions: RELATED references a column in a table that is itself a fact table
the source table is also a fact table
a proper Customer dimension does not exist

fixed_dax: Extract Customer dimension from Orders into DimCustomer. Use RELATED(Customer[CustomerName]) from Sales.

pattern: Calculated table used as a fact table — calculated tables refresh fully on every dataset refresh

conditions: result is a calculated table with fact-table row count
used as primary fact source in visuals
no incremental refresh available for calculated tables

fixed_dax: Add Margin as a calculated column in the source Sales table via Power Query, not as a calculated table.

pattern: Date table range does not cover all fact table dates — time intelligence returns blank for out-of-range dates

conditions: CALENDAR end date is earlier than MAX(FactTable[Date])
or CALENDAR start date is later than MIN(FactTable[Date])
time intelligence measures return BLANK for dates outside range

fixed_dax: DateTable = CALENDAR(MIN(Sales[OrderDate]), MAX(Sales[OrderDate]))

pattern: RLS role filter on a dimension with bidirectional relationship to fact — security breach risk

conditions: RLS filter is on a dimension table
dimension has a bidirectional relationship to the fact table
bidirectional filter propagates from dimension to fact AND back, potentially leaking data

fixed_dax: Apply RLS filter on the fact table directly, or change relationship to single-direction from dimension to fact.

pattern: Calculated column contains IF(YEAR(TODAY())=...) — value changes annually without dataset refresh

conditions: TODAY() inside a calculated column
calculated column is computed once at refresh time
value becomes stale if dataset is not refreshed at year boundary

fixed_dax: Use a measure instead: Is Current Year = IF(YEAR(MAX(Sales[OrderDate])) = YEAR(TODAY()), 1, 0)

pattern: Hardcoded date literal in a measure — does not respond to slicer or report filter

conditions: filter argument contains a hardcoded numeric year literal
no SELECTEDVALUE or slicer-driven parameter used
measure becomes incorrect in subsequent years

fixed_dax: Current Year Revenue = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESYTD('Date'[Date]))

pattern: Power Query step count > 20 — high risk of query folding break and full in-memory processing
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query M code with > 20 applied steps

conditions: M query has more than 20 applied steps
source is a relational database that supports folding
step chain includes List.Generate, Table.Buffer, or custom functions that break folding

fixed_dax: Consolidate transformation steps. Move filtering and column selection as early as possible. Use View Native Query to verify folding.

pattern: List.Generate in Power Query — never folds to the data source, always executes in Power Query engine
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query M using List.Generate

conditions: M code contains List.Generate
source is a foldable relational database
data volume is large and folding is expected

fixed_dax: Replace List.Generate with a SQL stored procedure, parametrized query, or Table.SelectRows on the native source.

pattern: Binary or image columns loaded in Power Query unnecessarily — inflates model size and refresh time
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query loads a Binary or image column

conditions: Power Query query includes a column of type Binary
column is not used in any visual or measure
model size is larger than expected relative to row count

fixed_dax: Remove the binary column in Power Query: Table.RemoveColumns(Source, {"ImageColumn"})

pattern: Power Query parameters not typed — treated as text, causes type mismatch in downstream filter steps
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query parameter has no type specified

conditions: parameter definition lacks explicit type annotation
parameter is used in a filter or join condition on a numeric or date column
implicit text type causes fold failure or runtime error

fixed_dax: Set parameter type explicitly: StartDate as date = #date(2020,1,1)

pattern: try...otherwise null in Power Query hiding data quality errors silently
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query step: try [Amount] otherwise null

conditions: try...otherwise null pattern in Power Query
nullified values are treated as valid blanks downstream
data quality issues are invisible in the model

fixed_dax: Log errors to a separate error table: Table.AddColumn(Source, "AmountSafe", each try [Amount] otherwise null) -- add error_audit query

pattern: Measure has no description metadata — breaks self-service BI and report documentation

conditions: measure definition has no associated description property
measure name is an abbreviation or not self-explanatory
model has more than 10 measures

fixed_dax: Add description in Power BI Desktop Properties pane: 'Total revenue from all posted invoices, excluding returns and cancellations.'

pattern: Table or column name matches a DAX reserved keyword — requires escaping in every DAX expression
bad_dax: DAX referencing column named 'Date' or table named 'Filter'

conditions: table or column name is a DAX function name or reserved word
examples: Date, Filter, All, Values, Calculate, If, Sum
results in parse errors or requires constant quoting

fixed_dax: Rename to 'OrderDate', 'SalesFilter', 'AllProductsTable', etc. via Power Query before loading.

pattern: Relationship key column is float/decimal type — floating point comparison failures cause missing rows
bad_dax: N/A — relationship defined on a Decimal Number column

conditions: relationship join column has Decimal Number or Fixed Decimal type
float comparison can fail for values that appear equal but differ at precision limit
missing rows in visuals despite matching values in source

fixed_dax: Change relationship key column to Whole Number or Text type in Power Query before loading.

pattern: Relationship on text column with mixed case — joins fail for rows where case does not match exactly
bad_dax: N/A — text column used as relationship key with mixed-case values

conditions: relationship join column is Text type
source data has inconsistent casing in key column
row count mismatch between fact and dimension after load

fixed_dax: Normalize case in Power Query: Text.Lower([CustomerCode]) on both sides before load.

pattern: Duplicate rows in dimension table — one-to-many relationship becomes many-to-many silently
bad_dax: N/A — DimCustomer has duplicate CustomerID rows

conditions: dimension table has non-unique values in the relationship key column
Power BI sets cardinality to many-to-many automatically
measure totals are inflated

fixed_dax: Deduplicate dimension key in Power Query: Table.Distinct(Source, {"CustomerID"})

pattern: Two fact tables share a dimension but there is no shared dimension — cross-fact filtering fails
bad_dax: N/A — FactSales and FactBudget both relate to a product dimension through separate columns

conditions: two or more fact tables reference the same logical dimension through different foreign keys
no single shared dimension table exists
filtering one fact does not propagate to the other

fixed_dax: Create a single DimProduct table. Relate both FactSales and FactBudget to DimProduct through ProductID.

pattern: Report page with no filters on a DirectQuery 100M+ row table — every visual issues a full table scan
bad_dax: N/A — report page has a visual connected to a DirectQuery fact table with no page filter applied

conditions: report is in DirectQuery mode
fact table has no date or key filter on the page or visual level
visuals load slowly or time out

fixed_dax: Add a default date slicer locked to the current month. Add a CALCULATE wrapper with default date filter in base measures.

pattern: Visual uses a high-cardinality axis (>200 members) on a bar or column chart — scrolling breaks insight
bad_dax: N/A — bar chart has a text dimension with >200 unique values on the axis

conditions: axis dimension has more than 200 unique values
visual type is bar chart, column chart, or line chart
user must scroll far to compare all values

fixed_dax: Limit to Top N using TOPN or a visual-level Top N filter. Show an 'Other' category for remainder.

pattern: Dataset has >8 tables with no display folders for measures — self-service users cannot find measures
bad_dax: N/A — all measures in flat list, no display folder property set

conditions: model has more than 8 tables
measure count exceeds 15
no display folder metadata on any measure

fixed_dax: Organize measures into display folders: Revenue, Cost & Margin, Time Intelligence, KPIs, Operational.

pattern: EARLIER() used in a measure — EARLIER is only valid inside calculated columns with nested row context

conditions: EARLIER appears in a measure definition
not inside a nested SUMX or FILTER that creates an outer row context within a calculated column

fixed_dax: Use VAR to capture the outer value: VAR Outer = MAX(Sales[Amount]) RETURN AVERAGEX(Sales, Sales[Amount] / Outer)

pattern: Multiple Excel sheet queries loaded separately without combining — increases refresh time and model complexity
bad_dax: N/A — Power Query has Sheet1, Sheet2, Sheet3 as separate queries instead of one combined query

conditions: multiple Power Query queries load from sheets of the same Excel workbook
schemas are identical or compatible
they are used in the same or similar visuals

fixed_dax: Use Excel.Workbook and Table.Combine to union all sheets in a single query: Table.Combine(List.Transform(ExcelFile[Data], each _))

pattern: CALCULATE with KEEPFILTERS and ALL in the same expression — KEEPFILTERS makes ALL redundant

conditions: KEEPFILTERS wraps ALL
KEEPFILTERS preserves existing filter context
ALL removes all filters first, then KEEPFILTERS re-adds them — net effect is identity

fixed_dax: Total = [Revenue] -- KEEPFILTERS(ALL(col)) is a no-op; remove both

pattern: RLS role that references a dimension table not loaded in the model — role fails silently or errors

conditions: RLS filter references a table that is excluded from the report's data model
or the table is hidden/removed after the role was created

fixed_dax: Verify the Manager table is loaded and related in the model. Test role using Power BI Desktop 'View as role'.

pattern: CALCULATE with REMOVEFILTERS(Table) removes all column filters including page-level and RLS filters

conditions: REMOVEFILTERS applied to a full table not a specific column
table has RLS roles defined
visual is in a report accessible by multiple security roles

fixed_dax: Grand Total = CALCULATE([Revenue], REMOVEFILTERS(Sales[Region]), REMOVEFILTERS(Sales[Category])) -- list only the columns that should be cleared

pattern: COUNTROWS(FILTER(FactTable, condition)) — use CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(), condition) instead

conditions: COUNTROWS wraps a FILTER on a fact table
the condition inside FILTER is a simple equality or comparison
no table-level iterator semantics are required

fixed_dax: Churn Count = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(FactSubscriptions), FactSubscriptions[Status] = "Churned")

pattern: TOPN without tiebreaker second sort column — tied values produce nondeterministic row inclusion
bad_dax: Top 3 Plans = CALCULATE([MRR], TOPN(3, ALL(DimPlan), [MRR], DESC))

conditions: TOPN has exactly 3 arguments (N, table, expression)
no secondary sort column is provided
result is used in a CALCULATE filter or SUMX — inclusion affects numeric result

fixed_dax: Top 3 Plans = CALCULATE([MRR], TOPN(3, ALL(DimPlan), [MRR], DESC, DimPlan[PlanKey], ASC))

pattern: USERNAME() used in RLS — deprecated in Power BI Service, silently returns UPN in some tenants and empty in others

conditions: USERNAME() appears in an RLS expression or measure
not wrapped with USERPRINCIPALNAME() fallback
target field is an email address

fixed_dax: [RLS Filter] = FILTER(DimEmployee, LOWER(DimEmployee[Email]) = LOWER(USERPRINCIPALNAME()))

pattern: RELATED() called directly in a measure body — requires row context; fails with 'RELATED can only be used in row context' error

conditions: RELATED is used inside FILTER iterating a fact table from a measure
the relationship exists but RELATED is being used in filter predicate without SUMX/AVERAGEX wrapper

fixed_dax:
Over Band = COUNTROWS(FILTER(FactHeadcount, FactHeadcount[Salary] > RELATED(DimJobLevel[SalaryMax])))
-- Note: RELATED is valid here since FILTER provides row context over FactHeadcount.
-- Fix: ensure the relationship from FactHeadcount to DimJobLevel is active.

pattern: LOOKUPVALUE inside SUMX when a direct relationship would allow RELATED — 10-100x slower
bad_dax: USD Amount = SUMX(FactGL, FactGL[Amount] * LOOKUPVALUE(DimExchange[Rate], DimExchange[Currency], FactGL[Currency]))

conditions: LOOKUPVALUE appears inside a row iterator (SUMX, AVERAGEX, etc.)
the lookup key matches a column that could be used as a relationship key
a direct relationship could replace the lookup

fixed_dax:
// Add relationship from FactGL[Currency] to DimExchange[Currency], then:
USD Amount = SUMX(FactGL, FactGL[Amount] * RELATED(DimExchange[Rate]))

pattern: DATEADD with -365 DAY for prior year — misses leap years, produces wrong date range

conditions: DATEADD offset is -365 or 365
interval is DAY
intent is to compare same period in prior year

fixed_dax: MRR PY = CALCULATE([MRR], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR(DimDate[Date]))

pattern: Multiple OR conditions on same column — IN operator is faster and more readable
bad_dax: Social Spend = CALCULATE([CTR], DimChannel[Platform] = "Facebook" || DimChannel[Platform] = "Instagram" || DimChannel[Platform] = "Twitter")

conditions: three or more OR conditions in the same CALCULATE filter
all OR branches test the same column against different literal values
IN operator would express the same logic more efficiently

fixed_dax: Social Spend = CALCULATE([CTR], DimChannel[Platform] IN {"Facebook", "Instagram", "Twitter"})

pattern: Hardcoded year literal in measure — will silently return wrong results when calendar year changes

conditions: a numeric year literal (e.g. 2023, 2024) is used as a filter argument
the literal is a hardcoded constant, not derived from a parameter or slicer
measure name or comment implies it is a 'current year' measure

fixed_dax: Revenue CY = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESYTD(DimDate[Date]))

pattern: RANKX over an entire fact table — should rank over a dimension or aggregated table

conditions: RANKX first argument is ALL() of a fact table (high row count)
ranking over the fact table produces one rank per fact row, not per dimension member

fixed_dax: Sales Rank = RANKX(ALLSELECTED(DimChannel[ChannelName]), [ROAS], , DESC, Dense)

pattern: SWITCH with duplicate condition value — second branch with same value is unreachable

conditions: SWITCH first argument is not TRUE()
two or more case values are identical strings or numbers
the second occurrence of the duplicate case can never be reached

fixed_dax: Work Category = SWITCH(FactWorkOrders[WorkType], "EM", "Emergency", "PM", "Preventive", "CM", "Corrective", "Unknown")

pattern: TODAY() in a calculated column — column evaluated at refresh time, stale between refreshes
bad_dax: Asset Age Years = DATEDIFF(DimAsset[InstalledDate], TODAY(), YEAR)

conditions: TODAY() or NOW() used in a calculated column definition
column stores age, duration, or days-since value
the value becomes incorrect between dataset refreshes

fixed_dax:
// Move to a measure:
Asset Age Years = DATEDIFF(MAX(DimAsset[InstalledDate]), TODAY(), YEAR)
